b:T16713,<div class="specification">
<p>The diagrams represent the structure of a protein before and after it has become denatured.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Dean Williams, L., 2019. <em>Molecular Interactions</em>. [online] Available at: https://ww2.chemistry.gatech.edu/~lw26/<br/>structure/molecular_interactions/mol_int.html [Accessed 20 August 2019].]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>State how many different types of amino acid there are, which can become part of a polypeptide when mRNA is translated.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline <strong>one</strong> cause of denaturation in proteins.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain how denaturation affects the activity of an enzyme.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/2/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>20;</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<ol style=\"list-style-type:lower-alpha;\">\n<li>increase in temperature/heat;</li>\n<li>change of pH;</li>\n<li>salt;</li>\n<li>heavy metals;</li>\n</ol>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<ol style=\"list-style-type:lower-alpha;\">\n<li>changes the shape of the (active site) of the enzyme;</li>\n<li>substrate would be unable to attach to the enzyme/active site;</li>\n<li>slows the enzyme activity / prevents reaction/catalysis from proceeding;</li>\n</ol>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Amazingly, most candidates missed gaining the mark for this question: a huge variety of incorrect numbers appeared, \"4\" was most frequent. Many candidates seemed to confuse the 4 nucleotide bases with the 20 amino acids.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>Most candidates answered these questions about enzymes either really well or very poorly. Some erroneous answers mistook the active site as being separate to the enzyme or part of the substrate. Another problem had to do with temperature as a denaturant. Lowering the temperature does not denature the enzyme, though it may slow the reaction down. The candidate needed to say increased or hot temperature to gain a mark.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
